learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the ability to work cooperatively, safely, ethically, inter-professionally and to communicate effectively to provide patient-centred care using a reflective approach.
Provide culturally safe and culturally competent practice that includes recognition of the distinct needs of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples in relation to oral health care provision
Accurately collect and record information that is necessary and relevant for patient care.
Maintain accurate, consistent, legible, and contemporaneous records of patient management, while protecting patient privacy.
Perform comprehensive examinations for health, disease and abnormalities of the dentition, mouth, and associated structures; and evaluate individual patient risk factors for oral disease.
Identify disease or abnormalities that require treatment or investigation and apply evidence, clinical reasoning, and judgement to plan appropriately sequenced oral health care.
Relate the impact of risk factors, systemic disease and medications to oral health and treatment planning decisions.

Effectively apply evidence, clinical reasoning and judgement to manage oral conditions, pathology, and medically related disorders and diseases associated with the dentition, mouth and associated structures.

assessments.task_description:
For further information, including instructions for each part and specific due dates, please see the Blackboard Task Sheet for this assessment item. In this task, students are required to complete and submit a Clinical Practice Portfolio over the course of the semester in order to document clinical performance within the patient clinic. Students must complete the portfolio for ALL clinical, dental assisting, fail to attend, and observational sessions.

To achieve a pass, the following conditions must be met:

Course-specific session sign-off forms


TASK DESCRIPTION: These forms are essential for tracking performance and must be completed at the end of every AM or PM session where you see a patient.

Submitted by the student: Session records and case items

Description: Students are required to submit a record of the item codes and clinical notes completed for each patient for the session. If no patient was seen, the Dental Assisting/FTA box must be selected.


Submitted by the Clinical Educator: Session performance assessment


Description: Clinical Educators assess the student under their supervision for the timetabled clinical session. Note, multiple sessions may occur in one day. Student marking expectations for each semester are calibrated to your year and stage in the DMD program. You are not being marked against the performance of a fully qualified dentist, but rather according to where you should be for your level of clinical training. Clinical educators will adjust their marking expectations accordingly as you progress throughout the program.

Session mark:

Each section of the rubric will be awarded either:

• Expectations met; OR

• Expectations NOT met.


The rubric consists of the following categories:

• Fitness to practice items (MUST PASS)

• History taking, examination and clinical information gathering

• Critical thinking, diagnosis and treatment planning

• Professionalism, communication and response to feedback

• Record keeping

• Time management

• Technical execution of treatment


End-of-semester portfolio review and case tallies

This task helps to reflect on clinical progress, feedback integration, and future growth.

The component includes two parts:

1. Case tallies for the semester AND a de-identified Patient Management System Treatment Report uploaded, and

2. An End-of-semester Reflection and Feedback Form.

Minimum performance requirement: There is no minimum number of case requirements.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The submitted Logbook must be COMPLETE in all the following respects and submitted by the published due date, which will then be assessed as part of this assessment task. Any incomplete or missing information can result in a failing grade in this must-pass assessment task. For further information, please see the Blackboard Task Sheet for this assessment item.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students are required to select a multidisciplinary case from patients managed in Semester 1 clinical work. The case selected should have elements of care planning/management from at least three distinct dental disciplines, such as Endodontics, Fixed/ Removable Prosthodontics, Oral Medicine, Oral Pathology, Orthodontics and Periodontics. 

The information from the managed case should be collated into sections within a PowerPoint template provided on Blackboard. This template walks the student through all required information to be collected and presented, including patient history, any presenting complaint/s, the clinical examination, diagnostic workup, the care plan, including a discussion of rationale and decisions specific to the components and sequencing of the plan and the delivery of care.

This PowerPoint will be submitted online to the course coordinator via Blackboard one week prior to the oral presentation date.

Students will be assessed by a panel of two examiners on four aspects of their selected case:

  • Clinical Information Gathering
  • Critical thinking, Diagnosis and Management Planning 
  • Clinical Treatment and Evaluation 
  • Discussion and Reflection 

Detailed information on task description, requirements for case selection, PowerPoint template and presentation details will be provided on the course Blackboard site in Semester 2.

This Oral Examination will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking, provision of feedback to the student, and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students must submit the PowerPoint presentation one week prior to the presentation via the Blackboard site for this course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to assess students' ability to apply clinical knowledge, skills, and decision-making in a structured, time-limited format across multiple stations. This examination evaluates competencies developed through pre-clinic activities, clinical sessions, and case-based discussions that directly apply to Professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist). 

 Tasks can include: (but not limited to)

  • Patient interview
  • History taking, including assessing pain experience
  • Reviewing case notes/ investigations to create a care plan
  • Communicating a care plan and obtaining informed consent
  • Communicating with carers, patients and families ethically, effectively and professionally
  • Working in professional clinical teams
  • Being aware at all times of ethical and legal obligations
  • Identifying at-risk situations for specific clinical scenarios
  • Writing a prescription within the scope of practice
  • Using instruments and materials within specific clinical scenarios for restorative, surgical and non-surgical situations, including delivering local anaesthetic and performing mock extractions on manikins.
  • Students must expect all routine clinical tasks to be included and should be up to date with therapeutic guidelines and medical emergency management in the dental situation.

 

Performance will be evaluated across four domains, which may vary by station depending on the task:

  1. Professionalism, Communication, and Teamwork
  2. Scientific Knowledge and Clinical Information Gathering
  3. Critical Thinking, Diagnosis, and Management Planning
  4. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation


This Examination will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking, provision of feedback to the student, and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Online case selection and care planning sessions

Students will have structured opportunities for online discussions and scaffolded support around selecting and documenting their cases. All sessions will be recorded in case students cannot attend due to placement commitments, and discussion boards will be utilised for further support.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08
